title: epipactis latifolia subsp. purpurata
intro: Epipactis latifolia subsp. purpurata, also known as the broad-leaved helleborine, is a sub-species of the orchid genus Epipactis. It is a type of terrestrial orchid that is native to Europe and Asia, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties, which can help to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The broad-leaved helleborine can be prepared as a tincture, infusion, or ointment, and is often used in herbal remedies to promote wound healing, reduce pain and inflammation, and support immune function.
